title: Magic numbers should be avoided
description: Magic numbers should not be usedAnalyze your codeintentionality-clearmaintainabilityCode Smellbrain-overloadA magic number is a hard-coded numerical value that may lack context or meaning. They should not be used because they can make the code less readable and maintainable.Why is this an issue?How can I fix it?Magic numbers make the code more complex to understand as it requires the reader to have knowledge about the global context to understand the number itself. Their usage may seem obvious when writing the code, but it may not be the case for another developer or later once the context faded away.
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

logic.triggering_conditions: Numeric literal (not 0, 1, -1) used directly in code
logic.reasoning: Detects magic numbers (hard-coded numeric values) that should be named constants. Excludes common values 0, 1, -1, and cases where the number is being declared as a const or readonly.
